# Readiness Checklist: Three-Layer Architecture Refactoring

**Purpose**: Validate whether the 005 requirements package is complete, clear, and implementation-ready for an execution handoff.
**Created**: 2026-03-17
**Feature**: [005-three-layer-architecture](../spec.md)
**Review Status**: Reviewed on 2026-03-17; unchecked items are intentional open requirement-quality gaps (not lost state).

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 - Are explicit acceptance requirements defined for all three layers (Application, Adapter, Common) rather than only high-level role descriptions? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001..FR-004]
- [ ] CHK002 - Does the spec define mandatory behavior when injected configuration is missing, partial, or malformed at startup? [Gap, Spec §FR-003]
- [ ] CHK003 - Are contract-level requirements specified for all user-visible operations (`search`, `fetch`, `list`) including inputs, outputs, and failure semantics? [Completeness, Spec §Key Entities; Contracts §adapter-protocol]
- [ ] CHK004 - Are requirements explicit about what "remove MCP-only dependencies from default build graph" includes and excludes? [Clarity, Tasks §T003]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK005 - Is "thin wrapper" for CLI quantified with objective boundary rules (what CLI may do vs must delegate)? [Ambiguity, Spec §User Story 1]
- [ ] CHK006 - Is "stable, developer-friendly API" defined with measurable quality attributes (naming, compatibility, error taxonomy expectations)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-010]
- [ ] CHK007 - Is the required error surface for `DocumentationError` precise enough to avoid inconsistent mappings across commands? [Clarity, Spec §FR-010; Data Model §DocumentationError]
- [ ] CHK008 - Is "App readiness" defined with measurable constraints (cache path policy, global-write prohibition scope, packaging expectations)? [Clarity, Spec §SC-008; Plan §Phase 3]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK009 - Do requirements remain consistent between "no shared cache by default" and later optional shared-cache locking behavior? [Consistency, Spec §Edge Cases; Spec §FR-008]
- [ ] CHK010 - Are version-compatibility requirements consistent across FR-013, FR-014, and gate criteria SC-009 without conflicting enforcement points? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013/FR-014/SC-009]
- [ ] CHK011 - Do contract expectations for async-only APIs align with task wording and CI gate intent? [Consistency, Spec §SC-007; Tasks §T002]
- [ ] CHK012 - Are responsibilities for authentication/session consistently excluded from Common/Adapter in all relevant sections? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006; Clarifications]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK013 - Can each gate SC-005..SC-009 be objectively evaluated from written requirements without relying on implicit team knowledge? [Measurability, Spec §SC-005..SC-009]
- [ ] CHK014 - Are pass/fail criteria defined for "zero logic duplication" in a way that can be checked repeatedly over time? [Gap, Spec §SC-002]
- [ ] CHK015 - Is the acceptance criteria for CLI standardized errors measurable (e.g., required categories, output shape, or mapping policy)? [Measurability, Spec §User Story 1; Contracts §cli-interface]
- [ ] CHK016 - Are framework/xcframework delivery requirements backed by measurable packaging outcomes and artifact expectations? [Measurability, Spec §FR-007; Tasks §T018]

## Scenario and Edge Coverage

- [ ] CHK017 - Are alternate flows documented for adapter initialization failures (version mismatch, invalid cache path, logger/config injection issues)? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases; Spec §FR-014]
- [ ] CHK018 - Are recovery requirements defined for partial failures where one data source is unavailable but others remain usable? [Gap, Coverage, Constitution §Offline-First]
- [ ] CHK019 - Are requirements explicit for test isolation scenarios where CLI/App tests must run without network or persistent disk side effects? [Coverage, Spec §FR-005; Tasks §T019-T020/T025]
- [ ] CHK020 - Are non-functional requirements for concurrency/thread-safety stated at requirement level, not only in implementation guidance? [Gap, Plan §Concurrency Model]

## Dependencies and Assumptions

- [ ] CHK021 - Are external dependency assumptions for CI gate execution documented (tooling availability, script location, enforcement trigger points)? [Dependency, Tasks §T002/T022]
- [ ] CHK022 - Is the assumption about future App platform scope (macOS/iOS/iPadOS) reflected consistently in contracts and type constraints? [Assumption, Spec §Clarifications; Spec §FR-007]
- [ ] CHK023 - Are prerequisites between phases expressed as enforceable readiness checks rather than only narrative dependency notes? [Completeness, Tasks §Dependencies]
- [ ] CHK024 - Is there an explicit requirement ID/traceability scheme linking FR/SC to tasks and checklist gates for auditability? [Traceability, Gap]
